concept_decisions.labels_compared: true transportability/generalizability methods vs external-validity generalizability appraisal vs ordinary randomized/cohort/review designs that only mention generalizability
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: manifest row plus all 73 assigned titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-008_RECORDS.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: A real transportability core is present, but many assigned papers are instead trial-eligibility/external-validity appraisals, predictive-model generalizability studies, or ordinary empirical studies whose only semantic link is a limitation statement about generalizability. Those structural questions should not remain merged.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: explicit agent-based or individual-based epidemic models vs microsimulation/decision-analytic models vs calibration/inference papers vs generic scenario analyses
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: manifest row plus all 649 assigned titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-008_RECORDS.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: The bucket contains a large genuine ABM/IBM core, but it also contains distinct individual-level microsimulation and cost-effectiveness papers, calibration/inference papers, and some broad scenario analyses. Those are structurally different design concepts.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: simulation studies of bias, coverage, power, ROC, and trial-design operating characteristics vs benchmark-threshold construction and diagnostic performance validation
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: manifest row plus all 35 assigned titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-008_RECORDS.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: Most assigned records are true operating-characteristic studies, but a minority are better understood as benchmark-threshold papers or applied diagnostic validation papers rather than simulations about estimator or design behavior.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Mechanistic dynamical transmission and disease-process models
concept_decisions.labels_compared: mechanistic transmission and compartmental models vs broader mechanistic disease-process models
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: manifest row plus all 37 assigned titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-008_RECORDS.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: The semantic core is mechanistic dynamical modeling, but the current name is too narrow because the set includes mechanistic biomarker, plant, vector, and ecological disease-process models in addition to classic compartmental transmission work.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: generic intervention or scenario simulation vs healthcare surveillance benchmarking vs benchmark/threshold construction vs method benchmarking vs empirical burden or metric studies
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: manifest row plus all 324 assigned titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-008_RECORDS.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: The current fallback label is not semantically admissible. The assigned papers resolve into several distinct structural questions, most prominently scenario simulation, healthcare benchmarking, benchmark/threshold construction, method benchmarking, and non-simulation burden or metric papers.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: explicit method or algorithm benchmarking/comparison vs diagnostic field validation vs benchmark-threshold construction vs healthcare benchmarking
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: manifest row plus all 22 assigned titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-008_RECORDS.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: A coherent comparison and benchmarking core exists, but several records are not method-comparison papers at all; they are diagnostic validations, benchmark-threshold papers, or healthcare benchmarking studies.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: simulation studies centered on structural bias, confounding, misclassification, or identification vs trial-design operating characteristics and decision-analytic scenario papers
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: manifest row plus all 9 assigned titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-008_RECORDS.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: The retained core is real and should stay distinct, but several assigned papers are actually Bayesian trial-design operating-characteristic studies or decision-analytic scenario models rather than structural-bias simulations.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: synthetic-data generation, benchmark datasets, and challenge resources vs calibration or inference methods for generative simulation models
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: manifest row plus all 24 assigned titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-008_RECORDS.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: Most assigned papers are genuinely about synthetic data, benchmark datasets, or modeling challenges, but a smaller subset is centered on calibration, pruning, or inference for agent-based or generative models and should be separated.

reassignments.rationale: Assesses whether trial eligibility criteria represent the real-world HIV/HCV coinfected population rather than developing transportability methodology.
A cohort-based appraisal of registration-trial generalizability, not a transportability method paper.
Scoping review of external validity and representativeness of bloodstream-infection trials.
Focuses on dataset shift and cross-population generalizability of a tuberculosis imaging model.
Independent multi-center external validation of a COVID-19 imaging AI model.
Systematic review of predictive-model performance and generalizability, not a transportability-method paper.
Primary design is a randomized antimicrobial-stewardship trial; generalizability appears only as a future-work remark.
Primary design is a propensity-score-adjusted retrospective cohort study rather than a transportability paper.
Qualitative cross-country program study; generalizability is incidental, not the design concept.
Development and evaluation of an ICU-admission prediction score, not a transportability method.
Centered on microsimulation cost-effectiveness analysis of HIV treatment.
Cost-effectiveness and screening model rather than an agent-based model.
Comparative cost-effectiveness screening analysis, structurally distinct from ABM/IBM work.
Decision-analytic model evaluation rather than agent-based epidemic simulation.
Core contribution is ensemble-based data assimilation for ABM inference.
Centered on neural parameter calibration for large-scale multiagent models.
Focused on calibration verification for stochastic agent-based disease-spread models.
Examines calibration improvement for an agent-based model rather than benchmark-resource creation.
Builds a surveillance benchmark for ICU-acquired infections.
Hospital infection-rate benchmarking within a surveillance system.
Explicitly about benchmarking and public reporting for infection control.
Defines benchmark risk thresholds for cervical screening management.
Introduces the SAAR as a standardized benchmark metric for antibiotic use.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Diagnostic assay validation and yield-comparison studies
reassignments.rationale: Field validation of the ReEBOV rapid test against RT-PCR benchmark.
Mixture-model estimation of seroprevalence is a biomarker or measurement-model paper, not generic simulation evaluation.
Global burden estimation and comparative metrics paper, not a simulation-method paper.
Burden and healthy-life-expectancy estimation study rather than methodological simulation.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Diagnostic assay validation and yield-comparison studies
reassignments.rationale: Field evaluation of an Ebola diagnostic assay against an RT-PCR benchmark.
Develops model-space selection for benchmark-dose estimation rather than benchmarking alternative methods.
Seeks a practical cleanliness benchmark rather than simulating estimator operating characteristics.

new_concepts.rationale: Needed for papers that evaluate representativeness, transport across sites, or external validation without developing transportability methodology itself.
Needed to preserve individual-level economic and screening models as a structural question distinct from agent-based interaction models.
Needed for papers centered on parameter inference, calibration, identifiability, data assimilation, or pruning of generative simulation models.
Needed for infection-control, antibiotic-use, and care-quality benchmarking papers that are not simulation-method studies.
Needed for papers that construct benchmark values, standardized ratios, internal thresholds, or acceptable-risk cutoffs.
Needed for ordinary randomized or cluster-randomized intervention papers incorrectly pulled in by generalizability language alone.
Needed for cohort and case-control effect-estimation papers that are not themselves transportability or simulation methods.
Needed for qualitative, commentary, review, and guideline-development papers whose design question is not simulation or transportability.
Needed for score, biomarker, and measurement-model development papers distinct from transportability or operating-characteristic simulation.
Needed for life-expectancy, burden, epidemic-transition, and control-metric estimation studies that are not benchmark-method or simulation-design papers.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Diagnostic assay validation and yield-comparison studies
new_concepts.rationale: Needed for field validation, sensitivity-specificity, and sample-yield comparison studies of diagnostic platforms.

concerns: The manifest and both manifested files were the only files inspected. The schema has no dedicated field for a full candidate_key-to-final-concept hash, so ordered_key_sha256 records the exact manifest candidate-key order only.

The provisional labels transportability_generalizability and simulation_methodological_evaluation are strongly heterogeneous; downstream integration should expect many candidate-level moves beyond the exemplar reassignments listed here.
